prompt: A cinematic black-and-white neo-western scene in heavy rain: a lone cowboy gunslinger walks directly toward the camera down a muddy road, centered in the frame, full body visible. He wears a dark wide-brim cowboy hat, a long soaked leather duster coat, dark shirt, dark pants, gloves, and boots, with rainwater glistening across every surface. His face is obscured in deep shadow for an anonymous, intimidating presence. In one hand he carries a revolver pointed downward. Behind him on the right side of the road stands or slowly follows a dark horse, also wet from the rain, slightly out of focus but clearly visible. On the left side of the road is a single wooden grave cross planted in the ground. The landscape is flat, barren, and desolate, with open fields stretching into the distance beneath low storm clouds. The road is filled with puddles and reflective wet mud, with visible splashes around the cowboy’s boots. Use dramatic storm lighting, high contrast, deep blacks, silvery highlights, and a gloomy, brooding atmosphere. Composition should feel like a dark western movie poster, symmetrical and confrontational, with the central figure dominating the image, rain streaks falling vertically across the whole frame, sharp texture on the coat and mud, shallow depth of field in the background, and an overall gritty, somber, menacing tone.
